# __Not released yet__

## Preliminary README generated by WindSurf SWE-1.5

# FWI - Full Waveform Inversion

A high-performance Julia implementation for seismic wave modeling and full waveform inversion, supporting both custom and SciML-based solvers with GPU acceleration.

## Overview

This project provides a flexible framework for seismic wave propagation and inversion, featuring:

- **High-performance finite difference stencils** with GPU support via KernelAbstractions
- **Multiple solver approaches**: Custom kernel-based and SciML ODE solvers
- **Anisotropic elastic wave modeling** supporting isotropic, VTI, and TTI media
- **Automatic differentiation** support via Enzyme and Zygote
- **Various boundary conditions**: Zero, periodic, and absorbing (planned)

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── Stencil.jl                 # High-performance finite difference stencils
├── read_model.jl             # Model reading utilities (SEGY format)
├── custom_solver/            # Custom kernel-based solver
│   ├── Model.jl              # Elastic model definitions and kernels
│   ├── PointSource.jl        # Point source implementations
│   └── TestModel.jl          # Model testing utilities
├── sciml_solver/             # SciML-based ODE solver
│   ├── Model.jl              # Abstract model types (Isotropic, VTI, TTI)
│   ├── Equations.jl          # Wave equation implementations
│   ├── BoundaryCondition.jl  # Boundary condition implementations
│   ├── NeuralPDEModel.jl     # Neural PDE model extensions
│   └── Solver.jl             # Solver utilities
└── tests/                    # Test suite
```

## Key Features

### Finite Difference Stencils (`Stencil.jl`)
- High-order finite difference operators with configurable accuracy
- GPU-accelerated kernels using KernelAbstractions
- Automatic differentiation support via Enzyme
- Optimized for both CPU and GPU backends

### Custom Solver (`custom_solver/`)
- Direct kernel implementation of elastic wave equations
- Leapfrog time-stepping scheme following Fichtner (2012)
- Support for anisotropic elastic parameters (ε, δ, γ)
- High-performance interior point updates

### SciML Solver (`sciml_solver/`)
- Integration with OrdinaryDiffEq ecosystem
- Flexible boundary condition system
- Support for multiple model types:
  - **IsotropicModel**: Homogeneous isotropic media
  - **VTIModel**: Vertically transversely isotropic media  
  - **TTIModel**: Tilted transversely isotropic media
- Modular equation system for strain and velocity updates

### Boundary Conditions
- **ZeroBC**: Dirichlet boundary conditions (zero values)
- **PeriodicBC**: Periodic boundary conditions
- **AbsorbingBC**: Absorbing boundary conditions (planned)

## Dependencies

- **KernelAbstractions**: GPU/CPU abstraction layer
- **CUDA**: GPU computing support
- **OrdinaryDiffEq**: ODE solvers from SciML
- **Enzyme**: Reverse-mode automatic differentiation
- **Zygote**: Forward/reverse automatic differentiation
- **FiniteDifferences**: Finite difference utilities
- **SegyIO**: SEGY format support for seismic data
- **MPI**: Parallel computing support

## Quick Start

### Basic Usage with Custom Solver

```julia
include("src/custom_solver/Model.jl")

# Create a simple model
Nx, Nz = 1000, 1000
Δx, Δz = 1.0, 1.0
Δt = 0.001

# Initialize fields
vx = zeros(Nx, Nz)
vz = zeros(Nx, Nz)
sxx = zeros(Nx, Nz)
szz = zeros(Nx, Nz)
sxz = zeros(Nx, Nz)

solver = Solver(vx, vz, sxx, szz, sxz)
```

### Using the SciML Solver

```julia
include("src/sciml_solver/Model.jl")
include("src/sciml_solver/Equations.jl")

# Create isotropic model
λ, μ = 30.0, 20.0  # Lamé parameters
model = IsotropicModel(λ, μ, Nx, Nz)

# Set up ODE problem
# (Implementation details depend on specific use case)
```

### High-Performance Stencils

```julia
include("src/Stencil.jl")

# Create 8th-order stencil
fdm = Stencil(8, 1.0)  # order, spacing

# GPU acceleration
using CUDA
u_gpu = cu(zeros(1024, 1024))
fdm_gpu = to_device(get_backend(u_gpu), fdm)

# Compute derivatives
du = similar(u_gpu)
ddx!(du, u_gpu, fdm_gpu)
```

## Performance

The stencil implementation achieves significant speedups on GPU hardware:

- **8th-order finite differences** with optimized kernels
- **Automatic differentiation** support for gradient-based inversion
- **Memory-efficient** in-place operations
- **Scalable** to large 2D/3D problems

## Development Status

This is an active research project for PhD studies in seismic imaging. Current development focuses on:

- [ ] Complete absorbing boundary conditions implementation
- [ ] Full waveform inversion workflows
- [ ] 3D extension
- [ ] Advanced source/receiver modeling
- [ ] Optimization for large-scale parallel computing

## References

- Fichtner, A. (2012). *Full Seismic Waveform Modelling and Inversion*. Springer.
- Implementation follows leapfrog time-stepping schemes for elastic wave propagation.
